Content:

Reduce admin time by 3 hours and 14 emails.

Hosts manage show production via Workflows.

title: Unicef x IRC - Podcast teasers - Albert Hofman
excerpts:
Section Title: Unicef x IRC - Podcast teasers
Content:
2D Animation Audio Design Design & Illustration Motion Design
Jul 2
Written By Albert Hofman
About this project
**Accelerated Sanitation and Water for All - Podcast series
UNICEF and IRC produced a series of podcasts. The series share stories from Burkina Faso, Niger, Nigeria, Mozambique and Tanzania, where experts from government and UNICEF speak about what works and what doesn’t. About water in relation to climate, gender equity, nutrition, finance and more.**
**UNICEF and IRC approached me to come up with a visual identity and a series of animated teasers for their new podcast series.**
Credits
Concept, design and animation: **Albert Hofman**
Sound design: **Bashel de Vries
** Additional animation: **Jofre Viaplana**
Teaser Burkina Faso
Albert Hofman https://www.alberthofman.com

title: How LSD Works Stuff You Should Know | iHeart
excerpts:
Section Title: How LSD Works > Episode Description
Content:
In 1943 Swiss chemist Albert Hofman discovered he'd created what may be the most potent hallucinogen known to humankind. Then he took a bike ride. Learn about the chemistry, neurology, history and cultural impact of LSD-25.
Learn more about your ad-choices at https://www.iheartpodcastnetwork.com
See [omnystudio.com/listener](https://omnystudio.com/listener) for privacy information.

title: Albert Hofmann and the Discovery of LSD
excerpts: In November 1938, Albert Hofmann November 1938, first created a derivative of ergot that would later be dubbed lysergic acid diethalyamide: LSD.

title: LSD, the CIA & the History of Psychedelic Science
excerpts: LSD, the CIA & the History of Psychedelic Science [UNSUNG HISTORY](https://www.unsunghistorypodcast.com/) In 1938, Swiss chemist Albert Hofmann accidentally developed the potent psychedelic LSD, although it would be several years before Hofmann realized what he’d created.
